title: Lotus wiedemannii
intro: Lotus wiedemannii, also known as Lotus stem, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to India. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Lotus wiedemannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Lotus stem is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or juice, and is also used in various herbal formulations such as tonics and tablets.
taxonomy: Lotus wiedemannii is a species of water-lily that belongs to the genus Lotus. It is a member of the family Nelumbonaceae, a group of flowering plants that are often found in aquatic environments. The classification of Lotus wiedemannii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. Additionally, it is classified under the order Proteales and the family Nelumbonaceae. This species is native to Australia and is considered to be an aquatic plant.
